article_title: Two Cybersecurity Professionals Face Up to 20 Years in Prison After Pleading Guilty to Running BlackCat Ransomware Attacks

content_preview.title: Two Cybersecurity Professionals Face Up to 20 Years in Prison After Pleading Guilty to Running BlackCat Ransomware Attacks
content_preview.summary: An incident response manager at Sygnia and a ransomware negotiator at DigitalMint admitted to moonlighting as ALPHV/BlackCat affiliates, targeting five US companies and causing over $9.5 million in losses.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Two cybersecurity professionals who used their industry expertise to conduct ransomware attacks against the very types of organizations they were hired to protect have pleaded guilty and face up to 20 years in federal prison. Ryan Clifford Goldberg, 40, of Georgia, and Kevin Tyler Martin, 36, of Texas, each admitted to one count of conspiracy to obstruct commerce by extortion for their roles as affiliates of the ALPHV/BlackCat ransomware operation, according to the [Department of Ju...

editor_notes.source_verification: CyberScoop confirmed Goldberg/Martin, Sygnia/DigitalMint, BlackCat, $9.5M, 5 companies, 20 years.
